name: Masala Vadai
description: Masala Vadai, also known as Paruppu Vadai or Dal Vada, is a crispy and flavourful South Indian fritter made from coarsely ground chana dal mixed with onions, herbs and spices. Unlike Medu Vada, which uses urad dal, Masala Vadai uses chana dal (split Bengal gram) giving it a distinctly crunchy texture and nutty flavour. It is a popular tea-time snack and street food across Tamil Nadu.

steps.instruction: Rinse chana dal well and soak in water for 2 to 3 hours. Keep covered.
Drain the water completely and set aside.
To a mixer jar, add ginger, garlic, fennel seeds and dried red chillies.
Add half of the soaked chana dal to the jar. Grind to a coarse mixture — it should be thick, not watery. A few whole dal pieces here and there give a crisp texture.
steps.tip: Do not add water while grinding the chana dal — the mixture must be thick and coarse.

steps.instruction: Transfer to a bowl. Grind the remaining chana dal the same way and add to the bowl.
steps.tip: A few whole dal pieces in the mixture are desirable as they give the vadai its signature crisp texture.

steps.instruction: Add chopped onion, coriander leaves, salt and a pinch of asafoetida.
Mix everything well. The masala vadai dough is now ready.
Take a small portion and shape into a ball. Slightly flatten it between your palms to form a thick disc.
steps.tip: Flatten the vadais slightly thick so the inside cooks through while the outside turns crisp.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a kadai. Check if ready by adding a pinch — it should rise immediately.
Add a few vadais at a time. Deep fry till golden brown, turning both sides.
steps.tip: Do not overcrowd the oil — fry in small batches for even cooking.

steps.instruction: Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with tea or coffee.
steps.tip: Masala Vadai is best served hot. It can be refrigerated and reheated in an oven or air fryer.

tips: Do not add water while grinding the chana dal — the mixture must be thick and coarse.
A few whole dal pieces in the mixture are desirable as they give the vadai its signature crisp texture.
Flatten the vadais slightly thick so the inside cooks through while the outside turns crisp.
Do not overcrowd the oil — fry in small batches for even cooking.
You can add 1 to 2 green chillies while grinding for extra heat.
Adding a little rice flour (1 tbsp) can make the vadai extra crispy.
Masala Vadai is best served hot. It can be refrigerated and reheated in an oven or air fryer.
